questions.question: Which anxiety disorder is characterized by recurrent, unexpected panic attacks along with persistent worry about having additional attacks?
questions.explanation: Panic disorder is defined by recurrent, unexpected panic attacks -- sudden surges of intense fear -- along with persistent concern about future attacks or their consequences. This distinguishes it from other anxiety disorders with more predictable triggers.

questions.question: A person who experiences excessive, uncontrollable worry about many different everyday concerns (finances, health, family) for at least six months most likely has:
questions.explanation: Generalized anxiety disorder (GAD) is characterized by excessive, uncontrollable worry about a wide range of everyday matters persisting for at least six months. Unlike phobias, which focus on specific triggers, GAD involves diffuse, free-floating anxiety across many domains.
